In 2012, Rust and Marty meet at a bar. Rust reveals he has been "functional but hammered" for ten years, eight of which he spent in Alaska working fishing boats and bars. He returned because "a man remembers his debts" — not a personal debt to Marty, but unfinished business from 1995.

Rust takes Marty to his storage unit, revealing an elaborate conspiracy board filled with photographs, documents, maps, and lattice structures. He theorizes the Dora Lange case connects to a broader network of crimes involving the Tuttle family, particularly centered on Shepherd's Flock preschool (which reopened as Light of the Way after child molestation accusations). Through interviews with former students, Rust identified a scarred man repeatedly mentioned by multiple witnesses. He connects regional traditions like Courir de Mardi Gras to the criminal network.

Rust reveals his most damning evidence: in 2010, he conducted illegal break-ins at Reverend Billy Lee Tuttle's properties, discovering photographs and a VHS tape. The tape shows young Marie Fontenot being led to an altar by masked adults in a ritualistic ceremony. This evidence convinces Marty to join Rust's investigation.

Marty visits his ex-wife Maggie, whose life has improved since the divorce. He thanks her and hints he may not return, suggesting the investigation is dangerous.

Working together, they access police records and interview contacts, discovering connections between the Ledoux family and an auto shop owner, that Marie Fontenot's missing persons file was mysteriously removed, and that Deputy Steve Geraci mishandled or concealed Marie's 1995 report. Sheriff Ted Childress knew Marie's family and may have suppressed the case.

Through Miss Delores, a former Tuttle family domestic worker, they learn the scarred man is likely a Tuttle family member — possibly named Childress or LeBeau — whose father burned his face as a child. Delores cryptically references "Carcosa" and "the Yellow King" when shown drawings of the lattices.

In a crucial scene, Detectives Papania and Gilbough unknowingly encounter the scarred man while asking for directions near an abandoned church. The man, mowing grass in a spiral pattern, tells them his family "has been here a long, long time." The detectives drive away, completely missing that the lawnmower man is their suspect — his shaved beard now reveals the scars on his face.

Rust confesses his true motivation: he intends to resolve this case before ending his life, describing existence as a meaningless "circle of violence and degradation." Rust and Marty confront the corrupt Deputy Geraci on a boat at gunpoint, escalating their pursuit of the conspiracy.
